questions.question: What wildlife protection and habitat conservation regulations apply in this area, and how are they enforced?
questions.answer: Not specified in the statute. The discovered source only indicates that a tree removal permit is required for trees within any wetland or within 150 feet of a wetland, on slopes exceeding 15%, for clear-cutting, for specimen trees, and for trees designated by the NYSDEC as protected native plants. It also states an exception for trees up to 100 feet from a dwelling that would fall naturally. No separate wildlife protection or habitat conservation enforcement mechanism is specified in the discovered statute excerpts.
vectorResearchSuggestions: Lewisboro tree preservation ordinance enforcement provisions
Lewisboro zoning code environmental protection enforcement
NY State DEC protected native plants regulations
